articles.headline: The Organ Doctors Ignored For Decades May Hold the Key to How Long You Live
articles.body: There is a small organ behind your breastbone called the thymus. Doctors largely wrote it off after puberty. Big mistake. Harvard and Mass General Brigham researchers used AI to analyze CT scans from over 25,000 adults and found that people with a healthier thymus had about a 50% lower risk of death and a 63% lower risk of cardiovascular death. The organ trains T cells, your immune system's front line. What hurts it? Chronic inflammation, smoking, and high body weight. The same stuff that ages everything else.
articles.takeaway: Keep inflammation low. Eat well, move daily, stay lean. Your immune system's command center depends on it.

articles.headline: Science Just Settled the Gym Debate: Stop Obsessing Over the Perfect Plan
articles.body: The American College of Sports Medicine just released its first major resistance training update in 17 years. They reviewed 137 studies covering more than 30,000 people. The verdict? The biggest gains come from simply going from nothing to something. Bands, bodyweight, barbells, it all works. You do not need to train to failure, use fancy equipment, or follow a complex program. What matters is showing up consistently and hitting all your major muscle groups at least twice a week. That is it.
articles.takeaway: Pick a routine you will actually stick with. Consistency beats complexity every single time.
articles.source: ACSM / Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise

product.name: Bowflex SelectTech 552 Dumbbells
product.blurb: Replace 15 sets of dumbbells. The best space-saving home gym investment.

articles.headline: Your Creatine Is Doing Something in Your Brain That You Probably Don't Know About
articles.body: Creatine is not just for your muscles. About 5% of your body's creatine is stored in the brain, where it helps power mental energy production. New 2026 research shows it may buffer the effects of sleep deprivation on cognitive function, support mood regulation, and protect against stress-related mental fatigue. One study found a high dose partially reversed brain metabolic changes after a sleepless night. Researchers flagged this as especially relevant for shift workers, first responders, and new parents.
articles.takeaway: If you are not sleeping enough or running hot on stress, creatine may be one of the smartest supplements on your shelf.

product.name: Thorne Creatine Monohydrate
product.blurb: The most researched supplement in history. Works for strength, brain health, and endurance.

articles.headline: Sitting All Day? A New Study Says Walking Can Undo Most of the Damage
articles.body: A massive University of Sydney study tracking over 72,000 people just dropped a finding that will make desk workers feel better about their mornings. No matter how many hours you sit, getting to around 9,000 to 10,000 steps a day cut mortality risk by nearly 40% and cardiovascular disease risk by over 20%. The good news for busy people? Even hitting 7,000 steps cuts your risk of early death significantly. You do not have to run. You just have to move.
articles.takeaway: Aim for 7,000 to 10,000 steps daily. A 20-minute walk after lunch gets you most of the way there.

product.name: Garmin Forerunner 255
product.blurb: The watch Chris trains with daily. Tracks every discipline without breaking the bank.

quote.text: Take care of your body. It's the only place you have to live.
quote.author: Jim Rohn
quote.context: American entrepreneur, author, and motivational speaker who influenced a generation of personal development thinkers.

workout.title: Sunday Reset: Full-Body Mobility Flow
workout.warmup: Start with 2 minutes of slow, deep belly breathing lying on your back. Then do 10 slow cat-cow movements on all fours to wake up the spine. Finish with 60 seconds of gentle hip circles standing to get blood moving into the joints.

exercises.name: Foam Roll Thoracic Spine
exercises.notes: Place the roller perpendicular to your spine at mid-back. Support your head with your hands. Slowly roll from mid-back to upper back. Pause on any tight spots. Do not roll the lower back.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch
exercises.notes: Sit with both knees bent at 90 degrees, one leg in front and one behind. Sit tall, hinge slightly forward at the hip. Switch sides. This targets internal and external hip rotation, which most desk workers desperately need.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.notes: Step into a deep lunge, place the same-side hand on the ground, then rotate the opposite arm up toward the ceiling. Move slowly and breathe. This hits your hip flexors, thoracic spine, and shoulders all at once.

exercises.name: Deep Squat Hold with Prying
exercises.notes: Hold a deep bodyweight squat with feet shoulder-width apart. Use your elbows to gently push your knees out. Hold a door frame or post if needed. This opens the hips and ankles over time.

exercises.name: Lying Glute and Piriformis Stretch (Figure Four)
exercises.notes: Lie on your back, cross one ankle over the opposite knee, and pull the bottom leg toward your chest. Breathe into the stretch. This targets the piriformis and outer glute, which tighten after long runs or sitting.

workout.cooldown: Finish with 3 minutes lying flat on your back in a full-body scan. Breathe slowly and consciously relax each body part from your feet to your face. Let your nervous system shift into recovery mode.

product.name: TriggerPoint GRID Foam Roller
product.blurb: Five minutes of rolling saves hours of stiffness. Non-negotiable on hard training days.

meal.title: Greek Chicken Power Bowl
ingredients: 6 oz cooked chicken breast, sliced or shredded
1/2 cup cooked brown rice or farro
2 tbsp tzatziki or plain Greek yogurt
meal.instructions: 1. If cooking chicken fresh, season with oregano, salt, and pepper and cook in a hot skillet with olive oil for 6 to 7 minutes per side until done. Slice thin. 2. Add warm rice or farro to the base of a bowl. 3. Layer chicken, tomatoes, cucumber, onion, and olives over the grain. 4. Top with feta and a generous spoonful of tzatziki. Eat immediately or pack for lunch.
meal.tip: Batch cook chicken breasts on Sunday and portion into containers. You will have protein ready for 3 to 4 lunches without any extra weekday cooking.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
